races.name: Volta Ciclista a Catalunya – Stage 6
races.description: The penultimate mountain stage and final summit finish of the Volta — finally delivering the stage last year's wind cancelled — 158km with 4,000m+ of climbing from Berga via the brutal special-category Coll del Pradell (14.6km, upper 5.5km averaging 11%) and Cat.1 Collada de Sant Isidre before the Cat.1 Queralt ascent (6km @ 7%) to the finish at the Sanctuary. Vingegaard dominates with a 57-second GC lead over Gall after demolishing all rivals on Stage 5; Pidcock crashed into a ravine on the Stage 5 descent and is effectively out of GC contention.

broadcasts.notes: Streaming on HBO Max (Discovery+ replaced from March 26). TV on TNT Sports channels. Plans from £25.99/month (Saver).
ASO race — exclusive to Peacock in the US. Premium $10.99/month.
Free-to-air on Spanish national public broadcaster.
Eurosport joins live from ~14:10 CET / 13:10 GMT on Saturday. Full earlier coverage on HBO Max depending on country.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 12:50 CET / 11:50 GMT confirmed by cyclinguptodate preview and IDL preview (12:45 CET also cited by cyclingstage — small variance, using the more widely-corroborated 12:50). Finish ~17:00 CET per cyclingstage route guide. Distance 158.2km / 158km confirmed by official Volta website and multiple sources. GC after Stage 5: 1. Vingegaard (VLB) 2. Gall (DCT) +57s 3. Martinez (TBV) +1:09 4. Lipowitz (RBH) +1:13 5. Paret-Peintre (SQS) +1:15 6. Evenepoel (RBH) +1:38 — confirmed by official Volta website and cyclingstage. Tom Pidcock crashed into a ravine on Stage 5's Collada Sobirana descent; dropped from 3rd to 74th in GC. João Almeida also crashed on same descent, recovered to join second chase group. Cyclingstage still lists Pidcock as Stage 6 favourite — this is outdated; he is not a GC factor.

races.name: Settimana Internazionale Coppi e Bartali – Stage 4
races.description: The penultimate stage crosses into Veneto and the UNESCO-listed Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco hills — a moderately selective day (profile score 67, 1,360m climbing) that threads through Treviso province to finish in the heart of Prosecco country; a stage where a breakaway is possible or the GC could shuffle ahead of Sunday's decisive finale.

broadcasts.notes: No live TV. Highlights on RaiSport HD from 16:25 CET / 15:25 GMT (tuttobiciweb lists Stage 4 highlights at 4:25 PM).
Free live stream from race start. Specific live video URL only published on race day — navigate to channel on race day.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 11:40 CET / 10:40 GMT confirmed by Ciclo21 live page. Finish ~15:35 CET per Ciclo21. Date March 28 confirmed by PCS stage profiles and all Italian sources. GC entering Stage 4: Axel Laurance (INEOS Grenadiers) leads overall — Stage 1 winner; Stage 2 won by Filippo D'Aiuto (General Store - Essegibi); Stage 3 won by Tommaso Dati. Cyclingnews stages page continues to show a one-day date offset error for all stages; all Italian primary sources confirm March 28 for Stage 4. tuttobiciweb schedule shows highlights at 4:25 PM for March 28.

issues: Volta Stage 6 start time: cyclingstage.com shows 12:45 CET; cyclinguptodate and IDL show 12:50 CET. PCS results page will confirm. Using 12:50 CET / 11:50 GMT based on more sources — a 5-minute variance.

Tom Pidcock Stage 6: Listed as favourite on some preview sources (written before Stage 5). In reality Pidcock crashed into a ravine on Stage 5's Collada Sobirana descent and dropped to 74th on GC. He is not a GC factor for Stage 6. Favourite list updated to reflect post-Stage 5 reality.
Volta Stage 5 finish time: stage was shortened by 2.2km on Coll de Pal due to wind; actual final climb was 16.7km not 18.9km, affecting elapsed race time.
Coppi e Bartali Stage 4 date: Cyclingnews stages page shows this stage as March 27 — the established one-day offset error. All Italian primary sources and PCS stage profiles confirm March 28.

Coppi e Bartali GC standings entering Stage 4: Axel Laurance (INEOS) leads overall based on Stage 1 win by Laurance, confirmed by PCS LiveStats feed which says 'Laurance Axel is the leader'. Full time gaps not confirmed at file generation time.

E3 Saxo Classic (yesterday, March 27): Van der Poel won hat-trick via 42km solo, nearly caught by 4-rider chase in final kilometre (Florian Vermeersch hesitated at key moment). Podium: 1. Van der Poel 2. Per Strand Hagenes 3. Florian Vermeersch.

Volta Stage 5 recap: Vingegaard solo win on Coll de Pal (shortened by 2.2km). Felix Gall 2nd (+50s), Lenny Martinez 3rd (+1:01). GC: Vingegaard leads Gall by 57s. Evenepoel dropped to 6th (+1:38). Pidcock crashed on descent into ravine, dropped to 74th. Dorian Godon (former race leader) dropped on final climb, fell to 67th.
